2604.24647.title: DepthKV: Layer-Dependent KV Cache Pruning for Long-Context LLM Inference
2604.24647.paper_summary: Long-context reasoning is a critical capability of large language models (LLMs), enabling applications such as long-document understanding, summarization, and code generation. However, efficient autoregressive inference relies on the key-value (KV) cache, whose memory footprint grows linearly with sequence length, leading to a major memory bottleneck. To mitigate this overhead, KV cache pruning methods discard cached tokens with low attention scores during inference. Most existing methods apply a uniform pruning ratio across layers, implicitly assuming that all layers contribute equally to overall model performance. We show that this assumption is suboptimal, as layers differ significantly in their sensitivity to pruning. We propose DepthKV, a layer-dependent pruning framework that allocates a fixed global KV budget across layers based on their sensitivity, rather than using a uniform allocation. Across multiple models and tasks, DepthKV consistently outperforms uniform pruning at the same global pruning ratio, demonstrating more effective utilization of the KV cache budget through layer-dependent allocation.
2604.24647.paper_summary_zh: 長期推理是大型語言模型（LLMs）的一項關鍵能力，使得長文檔理解、摘要和代碼生成等應用成為可能。<lb/>然而，高效的自回歸推理依賴於關鍵-值（KV）緩存，其內存佔用隨著序列長度線性增長，導致了主要的內存瓶頸。<lb/>為了減輕這一開銷，KV 緩存修剪方法在推理過程中丟棄低注意力分數的緩存標記。<lb/>大多數現有方法在各層之間應用統一的修剪比例，隱含地假設所有層對整體模型性能的貢獻是相等的。<lb/>我們展示了這一假設是次優的，因為各層對修剪的敏感性存在顯著差異。<lb/>我們提出了 DepthKV，一種層依賴的修剪框架，根據各層的敏感性分配固定的全局 KV 預算，而不是使用統一的分配。<lb/>在多個模型和任務中，DepthKV 在相同的全局修剪比例下始終優於統一修剪，通過層依賴的分配展示了 KV 緩存預算的更有效利用。
2604.24647.author: Zahra Dehghanighobadi et.al.
